Original_Search_Title: Food Demand Down
Headline: Uganda: Food Demand Down
Body_Text:
Kampala — THE demand for staple foodstuffs has reduced, forcing prices down, a weekly survey has revealed.
A bunch of matooke was sold at sh7,000 from sh10,000 at Nakasero Market. A kilogramme of maize flour went for sh600 from sh700, while beans were sold at sh500 from sh700.

Original_Search_Title: Bush Approves Sh7.7 Billion New Aid for Congo
Headline: Kenya: Bush Approves Sh7.7 Billion New Aid for Congo
Body_Text:
Nairobi — At least $52 million (Sh3.588 billion) annually for fiscal years 2006 and 2007 will be provided to the Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC) under legislation signed last week by President George W. Bush.
These funds are intended to enhance the United States' and international organisations' efforts to fight political, social and economic challenges facing the central Africa nation.

Original_Search_Title: NDDC Moves to Stop Youth Restiveness in N/Delta
Headline: Nigeria: NDDC Moves to Stop Youth Restiveness in N/Delta
Body_Text:
Lagos — The Niger Delta Development Commission (NDDC) has embarked on several youth empowerment schemes that would drastically reduce youth restiveness in the Niger Delta region.
Ambassador Sam Edem, Chairman of the NDDC told media executives at an end-of-year dinner in Lagos that the commission had just started the full implementation of the Niger Delta Regional Master Plan geared towards the massive generation of jobs and provision of infrastructure in the oil-rich region.

Original_Search_Title: Where Eight Or Ten Are Gathered...
Headline: Uganda: Where Eight Or Ten Are Gathered...
Body_Text:
Kampala — ... Nsenene is in their midst. If you find more than 20 people gathered in a brightly lit bushy area at midnight, the reason is most likely, nsenene. Whether in Masaka or Nabisunsa, wherever the grasshoppers land, they attract a large crowd, including children and adults
If you find more than 20 people gathered in a brightly lit bushy area at midnight, the reason is most likely, nsenene. The nsenene, an edible type of grasshopper, reach full maturity and fly out of the ground in the rainy season, which occurs twice a year from April to May and October through November in Uganda.

Original_Search_Title: New Law Enhances US Efforts to Assist, Provide Funds to Congo
Headline: Congo-Kinshasa: New Law Enhances US Efforts to Assist, Provide Funds to Congo
Publisher: United States Department of State (Washington, DC)
Body_Text:
Washington, DC — At least $52 million annually for fiscal years 2006 and 2007 will be provided to the Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC) under legislation signed December 22 by President Bush. These funds are intended to enhance the United States' and international organizations' efforts to fight political, social and economic challenges facing the African nation.
The act provides for bilateral assistance programs and establishes U.S. political, social, civil and economic policy objectives for the DRC, a country that earlier in 2006 held its first fully democratic election since 1960. (See related article.)

Original_Search_Title: Rebuilding the Breadbasket After the Conflict
Headline: Congo-Brazzaville: Rebuilding the Breadbasket After the Conflict
Body_Text:
Rome — IFAD’s latest programme in the Congo is set to start in December 2006 around the town of Dolisie in the south-west. During the civil wars in the 1990s the region was the scene of widespread destruction.
Agricultural production capacities were ruined. Three quarters of the livestock had either been killed or looted. There had been massive movements of civilians fleeing to the capital, Brazzaville, and to Pointe Noire, the main port on the Atlantic coast.

Original_Search_Title: Setting Up Farmers' Field Schools for Cassava Producers
Headline: Cameroon: Setting Up Farmers' Field Schools for Cassava Producers
Body_Text:
Rome, Italy — With public demand for cassava rising rapidly in Cameroon, new field schools to improve farmer productivity have been set up across the country over the past two years by the Roots and Tubers Market-Driven Development Programme (PNDRT).
Over 300 farmers were trained in 2005 to use a new technology package that helps to increase production and protect crops.

Original_Search_Title: Aggrieved Farmers Threaten to Take Over Secondary School
Headline: Ghana: Aggrieved Farmers Threaten to Take Over Secondary School
Body_Text:
Twifu Praso — ABOUT 65 Aggrieved farmers of Twifu Praso in the Central Region whose lands were affected by the acquisition of new site by government to elevate Twifu Praso Secondary School into a model one, are threatening to take over the school in a "military style" for lack of compensation.
Speaking to The Chronicle last Friday at Twifu-Praso, Mr Mark Abraham, the Secretary of the affected farmers, Nana Assiedu III, Gyasehen of Twifu Traditional Area, Thomas Kwesi Effah, landlord and a farmer as well as Kwabena Agyei-Boafo, landlord and victim, said they were ready to prevent students from occupying the new school when school reopens on January 16, 2007 because government had not paid an amount of ¢1.769 billion due them.

Original_Search_Title: Leaders in South Sign New Performance Contracts
Headline: Rwanda: Leaders in South Sign New Performance Contracts
Body_Text:
Kigali — As the year draws to the end, local leaders in the southern province have intensified the signing of new performance contracts aimed at guiding them on service-delivery next year.
On Sunday, December 17, after reviewing the achievements registered during the year 2006, the leaders discussed modalities of overcoming the challenges in 2007 and signed it as their guiding performance contract.

Original_Search_Title: Bugesera Food Problem to Be Solved
Headline: Rwanda: Bugesera Food Problem to Be Solved
Body_Text:
Kigali — Farmers in Bugesera District, Eastern Province have pledged to end the food shortage in the area.
Speaking to The New Times last week, Odette Mukankuranga, the Coordinator of Cooperative Inyingi y'ubuhinzi, disclosed various initiatives like rice-growing, aimed at reversing the problem. With 948 active members of the cooperative, the Kibaza wetland was reclaimed in 2003, with the help of World Food Programme (WFP). It has since managed to produce 240 tonnes of rice per year from only thirty hectares, Mukankuranga said.

Original_Search_Title: MP Spends ¢398 Million On Projects
Headline: Ghana: MP Spends ¢398 Million On Projects
Body_Text:
The Member of Parliament (MP) for the Kpone-Katamanso Constituency, Hon. Nii Laryea Afotey Agbo, a.k.a. Lion has undertaken a number of development projects throughout the constituency with his share of the HIPC and GETFund funds.
According to him, he has spent about ¢398 million on the various projects he has undertaken in his constituency between January and December, this year.

Original_Search_Title: Biofuel - Nigeria Needs 7bn KG of Cassava Annually
Headline: Nigeria: Biofuel - Nigeria Needs 7bn KG of Cassava Annually
Body_Text:
The ambition by Nigeria to achieve 10 percent ethanol for fuel can only be attained when production of cassava grows to about 7 billion kilograms annually, a research has shown.
The International Institute of Tropical Agriculture (IITA), Ibadan which revealed the outcome of a research recently said the 10 percent attainment of Ethanol in fuel would require massive production of cassava and sugarcane.

Original_Search_Title: Tens of Thousands of Villagers On the Run
Headline: Central African Republic: Tens of Thousands of Villagers On the Run
Body_Text:
Kaga Bandoro — Around Dikba Elisi's oldest child's neck is a plastic whistle, so that if the family is forced to flee into maize fields and the children get lost, the adults have a better chance of finding them.
Elisi, her three young children and sister, are living under two pieces of tin they salvaged, sleeping on a single bed, hidden in swaying fields three kilometres behind their old village. Another attack, she said, was unlikely - there was nothing left to burn or steal since the family's straw-roofed shack was razed to the ground in mid-November and all the family's furniture, food and money were stolen.

Original_Search_Title: Why Nigeria Must Be Africa's Financial Hub-Soludo
Headline: Africa: Why Nigeria Must Be Africa's Financial Hub-Soludo
Body_Text:
Central Bank of Nigeria (CBN) Governor, Professor Charles Soludo, has said that the goal of the Financial System Strategy 2020, which is to make Nigeria Africa's financial hub, is important to sustaining the nation's economy.
Speaking, last week, on "Financial System Strategy 2020: The Next Development Frontier", at the second edition of NTA annual public lecture series, Soludo observed that, "as Africa's potential largest economy, it needs to be the dominant financial services market for sustainable economy."

Original_Search_Title: Why is Sharing So Scaring These Days?
Headline: Uganda: Why is Sharing So Scaring These Days?
Body_Text:
The one image I know will not leave my mind for a while is that of the little boy (R.I.P) and his grandmother who were left to starve to death somewhere in Bushenyi. Most likely, by now most people have forgotten all about this boy and they have gone back to their daily lives, eating chunks of pork and enjoying beer every evening, after all they can afford it. It beats me when I think about how we can be so uncaring.
Whatever happened to the African spirit of helping? Are people so hard up even in the villages that no one will spare an old starving lady and her grandson a tuber or two of cassava or potatoes and some beans?

Original_Search_Title: Posho is One of the Reasons Africans Have Remained Dull
Headline: Uganda: Posho is One of the Reasons Africans Have Remained Dull
Body_Text:
Kampala — EVER since Vice-President Gilbert Bukenya criticised maize as a staple food in schools, several people have raised dust about his statement. Head teachers claim that change of menu will mean a hike in school fees. Other people have doubted Prof. Bukenya's nutritional education.
I am neither a nutritionist nor a head teacher, but a parent whose children feed on posho and beans daily in school.

Original_Search_Title: Food Security . . . Taking Africa Out of the Hunger Pit
Headline: Africa: Food Security . . . Taking Africa Out of the Hunger Pit
Body_Text:
Lagos — The role of agriculture in addressing the continent's economic development is a foregone conclusion. Business as usual will not improve food productivity on the continent. Africa needs to change its ways in order to be able to feed its people and ensure its main source of economic development - agriculture - grows and develops. Prosperity by the farmer will influence economic growth and the management and handling of other factors such as infrastructure. Innovative agriculture, acquired through novel relationships with technology developers, and developed through mutually satisfactory relationships with partners in research, development and commercialisation, should lead to prosperity for the smallholder farmer, to food security and economic growth for Africa.
Sixty-eight-year-old Mr. Chijioke Ibekwem (not real name) has known no other occupation since his childhood apart from farming. As soon as the first signs of rain are noticed every year, he gets his family ready for farming. While Egonma, his wife of 40 years and their three young children will concentrate on maize, cassava, melon ad other such crops that ar regarded as fit only women, Chjioke concentrates on yam, which the Igbo regard as the king of crops. Year after year the Ibekwem family will till the same land, cultivate the same crops, use the same methods and get almost the same results.

Original_Search_Title: Beware of China
Headline: Nigeria: Beware of China
Body_Text:
Lagos — China is a dangerous nation. One of the last bastions of communism, the country's despots must be given credit for managing to somehow convince the rest of the world that they are an okay communist nation. While the two other remaining communist outposts, North Korea and Cuba, groan heavily under the weight of sanctions and have attained international pariah status, China has thrived economically and politically. It has done this through shrewd planning and realpolitik. China is now widely seen as the successor to the U.S.S.R in the world that is increasingly becoming bipolar. It is also an emerging economic power. And despite the country's penchant for oppressing its neighbours and its citizens-it invaded Tibet, supports North Korea's regime, interferes in Cambodia and never misses the opportunity to intimidate Taiwan by staging mock invasions and issuing inflammatory statements-the International Olympic Community overlooked all these crimes and awarded them the right to host the 2008 Olympics. This is China-a country that clearly understands international politics is not about playing fair.
The usually circumspect United States of America is so enamoured with China that it rewards its abysmal human rights record by celebrating the entry of U.S companies into China while they turn a blind eye to China's human rights abuses.

Original_Search_Title: Agric Minister Worried About Food Insufficiency
Headline: Ghana: Agric Minister Worried About Food Insufficiency
Body_Text:
Kumasi — THE MINISTER of Food and Agriculture, Mr. Ernest Debrah has expressed grave concern about Ghana's inability to feed itself although 60% of the population are farmers which situation he says is responsible for 2.4 million of the population being malnourished.
He lamented that as an agricultural country with most of scholarships specializing in the field, it was worrisome that Ghana was still not sufficient in food and was rather using huge sums of monies to import food at he expense of developmental projects.

Original_Search_Title: My Blueprint to a Greater Nigeria - Gov. Adamu
Headline: Nigeria: My Blueprint to a Greater Nigeria - Gov. Adamu
Body_Text:
Lagos — My Call to National Service- Being Text of a Speech by His Excellency, Dr. Abdullahi Adamu, Executive Governor of Nasarawa State and Presidential Aspirant Under the Umbrella of the Peoples Democratic Party at the Public Presentation of The Bridge to a Greater Nigeria Presidential Campaign Manifesto, Shehu Musa Yar'Adua Center, Abuja, Wednesday, December 06, 2006
About Seven weeks ago, I officially collected a form to indicate my interest in running for president in next year's general elections on the platform of my party, the Peoples Democratic Party, PDP. I now reaffirm that quest as a humble, privileged patriot.

Original_Search_Title: Plan for 2007 Presented to Parliament
Headline: Mozambique: Plan for 2007 Presented to Parliament
Body_Text:
Maputo — The Mozambican government on Wednesday presented the country's parliament, the Assembly of the Republic, with an economic and social plan for next year, characterised by continued high growth, and relatively low inflation.
The Planning and Development Minister, Aiuba Cuereneia, said that the target for 2007 is a growth rate of seven per cent.

Original_Search_Title: Food Crisis Looms in the North, East-Central
Headline: Burundi: Food Crisis Looms in the North, East-Central
Body_Text:
Bujumbura — A food crisis is looming in the north and east-central Burundi, a consultant at the United Nations Food and Agriculture Organization (FAO) has said.
However, Methode Niyongendako said the severity of the food crisis in Ngozi, Kirundo, Kayanza, Muyinga, Karuzi and Cankuzo provinces would not be as bad as last year.

Original_Search_Title: Preliminary Results Show Ravalomanana Victory
Headline: Madagascar: Preliminary Results Give Ravalomanana Victory in Madagascar Polls
Body_Text:
Marc Ravalomanana is poised for a second term as Madagascar's president as preliminary results point to a comfortable lead for the incumbent leader in 3 December presidential polls.
Preliminary results from the Ministry of Interior and Administrative Reform (MIRA) gave Ravalomanana 55 percent against around 12 percent for his nearest rival, Jean Lahiniriko. Lahiniriko is a former speaker of the National Assembly.

Original_Search_Title: Graduate Finds Gold in Farming
Headline: Uganda: Graduate Finds Gold in Farming
Body_Text:
Kampala — A LOOK of contentment crosses Kaggwa Kasolo's face when you ask him what he has achieved from agriculture.
"I have achieved a lot since I started farming in 2001," he asserts.

Original_Search_Title: Farming Briefs
Headline: Uganda: Farming Briefs
Body_Text:
Kampala — No market
KAMPALA - The Government should take a lead role in supporting the private sector in the development of markets for agricultural produce, the National Agricultural Advisory Services (NAADS) board chairman Dr. David Kazungu has said. "NAADS supports enterprise development, which has led to increase in production but without a growing market demand or organised market to absorb the produce," said Kazungu. He was speaking at the sixth NAADS-Government and donor review meeting at Hotel Africana recently. He said many farmers are stuck with their produce.

Original_Search_Title: MTN Paints Scribes Yellow
Headline: Uganda: MTN Paints Scribes Yellow
Body_Text:
It has become a norm for MTN Uganda to host the media at a "yellow party" every year. Scribes who turned up for this year's party last week were convinced this was the highlight of their nightlife on their social calendar.
Time keeping was good and by 9 p.m. Ange Mystique was filled with all categories of journalists; editors, newsreaders, reporters, salesmen and needless to say, a few flukers.

Original_Search_Title: Two Billion-Cedi Milk Processing Factory for Juapong
Headline: Ghana: Two Billion-Cedi Milk Processing Factory for Juapong
Body_Text:
A TWO billion cedi Milk Processing Factory is being established at Juapong in the North Tongu District of the Volta Region to convert cattle products into various forms of food for both local and international consumption.
The establishment of the factory, which was almost completed, had the relevant structure, put up as well as equipped with the necessary machines after a feasibility study was conducted on the viability of the factory.

Original_Search_Title: Shell Donates N35m to Six Communities
Headline: Nigeria: Shell Donates N35m to Six Communities
Body_Text:
Warri — SHELL Petroleum Development Company (SPDC) has donated over N35million to six rural and riverine communities in Warri South, South- West, Isoko North and Ekeremo local government councils of Delta and Bayelsa States.
The amount covers four 16 seater Hiace buses donated to Kokodiagbene and Angalaweigbene communities; Micro-credit loan to Kantu, Omadino and Kokodiagbene communities; a rice processing mill to Ayamassa community and a Cassava mill valued at N2,437,940 to the Ovrode community respectively.

Original_Search_Title: Shell Donates N35m to Six Rural Communities
Headline: Nigeria: Shell Donates N35m to Six Rural Communities
Body_Text:
Warri — IN demonstration of care and good neighbourliness, the Shell Petroleum Development Company (SPDC) has donated over N35million to six rural and riverine communities in Warri South, South- West, Isoko North and Ekeremo local government councils of Delta and Bayelsa States.
The amount covers four 16- seater Hiace buses donated to Kokodiagbene and Angalaweigbene communities; Micro-credit loan to Kantu, Omadino and Kokodiagbene communities; a rice processing mill to Ayamassa community and a cassava mill valued at N2,437,940 to the Ovrode community respectively.

Original_Search_Title: Food for the Soul: Something Fruity And Healthy This Season
Headline: Botswana: Food for the Soul: Something Fruity And Healthy This Season
Body_Text:
Gabarone — December is already here! This season means a lot of things to many people; vacation, family re-unions, moments of reflection, worship, and so on. But what always stands out in epic proportions is the amount of food and drink consumed at this time of the year. I would say eating instantly becomes a favourite sport over the holiday season, much to the regret of many a folk when the New Year rolls around - with the guaranteed extra kilos and rolls of course. Oh well that is the price we have to pay for the gluttony we blindly display.
I suppose one way to get around this self-inflicted disaster is to balance the diet out by eating more fruits. And this is the right season with juicy mangoes, papayas, and other tropical varieties. Make salads, add them to your morning cereals, eat them whole as snacks or dessert, use them as pancake or flapjack fillings, the list is endless. Just be creative and you will be amazed at how calories you can save, and how healthy you will look and feel. Fruits add a lot of value from a variety, taste, and nutritional point of view.

Original_Search_Title: Have the Reforms Reformed Us?
Headline: Nigeria: Have the Reforms Reformed Us?
Body_Text:
Lagos — "Have all these reform measures translated to the alleviation or eradication of poverty among poor Nigerians? The definitive answer is NO! Poor Nigerians have had it hard on them because we have a government that care for the rich and do not consider the plight of the common man. For an administration that has increased the price of petroleum products five times in six years without due consideration of involving the masses in a dialogue is most shameful and sad. With all the reforms going on, privatisation of government companies, where government officials use 'fronts' in buying over government companies, and one man wants to own all syndrome which we are told is quietly causing the feud between the two top men in Abuja, is annoying. Much for a President who does know that price of 'garri', the staple food of the common man in Nigeria, is most disturbing. We are trapped firmly in the period of big man politics, a politics founded totally on individuality.Yes I do agree that 'Nigerians have not significantly benefited from these reforms not because the reforms are fundamentally flawed but simply because of the socio-political and institutional weaknesses of the Nigerian state.
The gains and dividends of reforms are concentrated in the hands of the ruling class, their cronies and some sections of the Nigerian elite. Take, for instance, the setting up of the corporation called TRANSCORP. To me, it is laudable but I do not see that company making it in time to come, for we know that Nigerians are good at starting and setting up companies and schemes. Like the Onwuka High Tech Company that today is moribund, sustainability will be the bane of that corporation - mark it! Though I am told that the major founders of the corporation belong to the clique called AGIP (Any Government In Power).

Original_Search_Title: Invest in Upland Rice, VP Tells MPs
Headline: Uganda: Invest in Upland Rice, VP Tells MPs
Body_Text:
Parliament — THE Vice President Gilbert Bukenya has called upon MPs to invest in what he described as the "lucrative" Upland rice business to help their constituencies fight poverty.
Bukenya was on December 7 speaking at an Upland rice sensitisation meeting at Parliament, where he said Upland rice has been identified as major tool in poverty eradication.

Original_Search_Title: Shell Spends N12.7 Billion On Devt
Headline: Nigeria: Shell Spends N12.7 Billion On Devt
Body_Text:
Port Harcourt — Shell Petroleum Development Company (SPDC), has said within 2005, it went into different developmental intervention efforts in the Niger Delta worth $97.5 million (about N12.675 billion), aimed at improving the lives of the people.
This was in addition to other empowerment programmmes in partnership with organisations which have similar developmental interventions.

Original_Search_Title: Help for Child-Headed Homes
Headline: Zambia: Help for Child-Headed Homes
Body_Text:
Zambia is grappling with the growing problem of thousands of child-headed homes, created by one of the highest HIV infection rates in the world.
Kapiri Mposhi, a commercial hub in central Zambia, about 200km north of the capital, Lusaka, has one of the highest levels of HIV prevalence in the country and a significant number of child-headed homes. UNAIDS estimates the national HIV rate at 17 percent.

Original_Search_Title: Ban Posho, Says VP
Headline: Uganda: Ban Posho, Says VP
Body_Text:
Kampala — VICE-President Prof. Gilbert Bukenya has criticised the use of posho (maize meal) as a staple dish in schools.
Bukenya, who was on Thursday meeting MPs from the upland rice growing areas urged that the eating of posho in schools be outlawed.

Original_Search_Title: Help for Child-Headed Homes
Headline: Zambia: Help for Child-Headed Homes
Body_Text:
Zambia is grappling with the growing problem of thousands of child-headed homes, created by one of the highest HIV infection rates in the world.
Kapiri Mposhi, a commercial hub in central Zambia, about 200km north of the capital, Lusaka, has one of the highest levels of HIV prevalence in the country and a significant number of child-headed homes. UNAIDS estimates the national HIV rate at 17 percent.

Original_Search_Title: Residents to Transform Farmlands
Headline: Rwanda: Residents to Transform Farmlands
Body_Text:
Muhanga — Plans are underway to transform farming activities through building terraces on farmlands for the residents of Cyeza, the Mayor of Muhanga district Augustine Hategeka has said.
In an exclusive interview with The New Time, Hategeka said the district in collaboration with Food for the Hungry International (FHI) will sponsor and monitor the three month project in which at least one hundred (100ha) hectares of terraces have to be prepared by the residents.

Original_Search_Title: Obj Recounts Efforts At Diversifying Economy
Headline: Nigeria: Obj Recounts Efforts At Diversifying Economy
Body_Text:
PRESIDENT Olusegun Obasanjo said in the past seven years, his administration has initiated several policies and programmes toward diversifying the economy from oil to non- oil exports.
The President has also applauded Odua Chambers of Commerce, Industry Mines and Agriculture (OOCIMA) for staging the ongoing Agro-Nigeria 2006 trade fair, at Lekan Salami Stadium, Adamasingba, Ibadan.

Original_Search_Title: Duke - I Don't Subject Myself to Captive Mentality
Headline: Nigeria: Duke - I Don't Subject Myself to Captive Mentality
Body_Text:
Lagos — One thing that has been said about Donald Duke is that, 'he is an oyinbo man, both names are English and always wearing suit.' I think I'm only being myself by my dress code which is what makes me comfortable. And I think it is practicable considering the work that I do. My name, my dress code are part of my heritage. I am not going to change that for anything. I want to be myself all the time.
Ok, why do you want to be President of Nigeria?

Original_Search_Title: Biosafety - Legislation to the Rescue?
Headline: Nigeria: Biosafety - Legislation to the Rescue?
Body_Text:
Lagos — Everyone of the farmers had harsh words for the company that introduced the genetically modified crops to them: "They lied to us about the crops and now we have lost everything", cried one. Another blurted out: "They have killed our crops and in frustration, more farmers are killing themselves through suicides after suffering huge losses". Yet another bemoaned: "They have put us under a permanent debt from which we cannot recover".
These statements were made by some Indian farmers, who had patronised Monsanto to plant genetically modified crops on their land, but instead of the expected high yield, they got virtually nothing in return for their efforts. Their views came from a video clip shown at the two-day Regional Conference on Biosafety held in Abuja last week.

Original_Search_Title: GMOs Are Key to Commercial Agriculture
Headline: Uganda: GMOs Are Key to Commercial Agriculture
Body_Text:
Kampala — THERE are many products and processes of biotechnology such as brewing of beer, in-vitro (or in test tube) fertilisation, plant tissue culture, among others, that have been developed and applied for many years to the benefit of mankind without much controversy.
However Genetic Modification Technology has caused controversy despite the fact that Genetically Modified Organisms (GMOs), which are products of products of biotechnology, have been commercialised in more than 40 countries with significant benefits.

Original_Search_Title: Bayelsa Pays School Fees for 100 Students
Headline: Nigeria: Bayelsa Pays School Fees for 100 Students
Body_Text:
Yenagoa — Bayelsa State government has promised to pay six years tuition for the 100 students on government/Shell scholarship placed in five different international schools in the country.
Governor Jonathan stated this when he visited the students in the schools and inspected facilities on ground.

Original_Search_Title: Agriculture Minister - Ghana is Now Self Sufficient In Food Production
Headline: Ghana: Agriculture Minister - Ghana is Now Self Sufficient In Food Production
Body_Text:
Nkawie-Ash — THE MINISTER of Food and Agriculture, Mr. Ernest Akubuor Debrah has stated that the New Patriotic Party (NPP) government, since assuming the reign of administration of this country in 2001, has worked so hard to bring about positive growth in the agriculture sector.
This, he added, is in line with the party's manifesto to pursue policies that will bring about positive change in the socio-economic and political fabric of the nation.

Original_Search_Title: Experts Propose Agro-Technical Aid Corps for Africa
Headline: Africa: Experts Propose Agro-Technical Aid Corps for Africa
Body_Text:
Abuja — A summit to address food security problems in Africa, started yesterday in Abuja, with delegates hoping to establish a pool of agricultural technical expertise from where countries in the continent that need them can draw.
"I think this is one of the major challenges that we would like to see addressed here," Professor Richard Mkandawire, New Partnership for Africa's Development (NEPAD) agriculture advisor said, adding that a number of summits had been held in the past to address food security problems in Africa.

Original_Search_Title: UN Needs Urgent Funds to Feed Thousands Fleeing Terror
Headline: Central African Republic: UN Needs Urgent Funds to Feed Thousands Fleeing Terror
Body_Text:
With violence, fighting and rebel attacks spreading terror and uprooting scores of thousands of people in the north-western Central African Republic (CAR), the United Nations World Food Programme (WFP) today called for urgent international funding and a presence on the ground to support its emergency feeding operations.
"The world must wake up to the reality and extent of the suffering here in Central Africa," WFP Country Director Jean-Charles Dei said, voicing particular concern for an estimated 150,000 people who are believed to be living in the bush, surviving on little more than the wild food that grows around them.

Original_Search_Title: Pest Management - a New Business With Great Potentials
Headline: Nigeria: Pest Management - a New Business With Great Potentials
Body_Text:
Lagos — Dr Godwin Oyediji, Managing Director of Godoye Enterprises Ltd., is a pest management who has been practising for over 22 years. He spoke to Okey Ndiribe on his experiences in this line of business.
DR. Oyediji got introduced into pest management practice when he worked with Boots Nig. Plc as an agro-chemical sales representative for pest control products and equipment as well as for veterinary drugs.

Original_Search_Title: Ayeekoo to Our Hard Working Farmers
Headline: Ghana: Ayeekoo to Our Hard Working Farmers
Body_Text:
Accra — The real heroes of our nation building efforts are the women and men who have sacrificed to till the land for food and brave the stormy sea for fish to feed the large majority of the population. But what do they have to show?
No doubt, many Ghanaian farmers celebrated last Friday's Farmer's Day with mixed feelings. For cocoa farmers, the day was definitely a joyous one, thanks to the recent upward adjustment of the producer price of the cocoa in particular.

Original_Search_Title: National Farmers Day
Headline: Ghana: National Farmers Day
Body_Text:
A thirty six-year-old man who can neither hear nor speak, Mr. Kojo Okanto is the Mfantseman District Best Farmer for 2006.
Mr. Okanto, from Ekumfi Nanaben and a product of Cape Coast School of Deaf received a bicycle, suitcase, radio cassette recorder, three bars of key soap, cakes of Geisha soap and three cutlasses as his prize at the Mfantseman's Farmers Day Celebration at Ekumfi Essuehyia on National Farmers Day last Friday.

Original_Search_Title: Need to Intensify HIV Campaigns
Headline: Zimbabwe: Need to Intensify HIV Campaigns
Body_Text:
Harare — THERE is need to intensify HIV and Aids awareness campaigns, particularly in rural communities, in order to do away with some misguided superstitious beliefs on the pandemic, Mashonaland East Governor Cde Ray Kaukonde said yesterday.
He said there were still some people who were attributing HIV and Aids-related deaths to witchcraft due to lack of proper information on how the disease was spread.

Original_Search_Title: What We Have Against This Budget - NDC
Headline: Ghana: What We Have Against This Budget - NDC
Body_Text:
"In 2007 about 31% of our budget is to be donor funded. Investement which is to propel growth, accounts for only 21.3% of budgetary expenditure. Out of the Total investment of 11 565.2 billion cedis only 2 598.9 cedis or 4.2% is financed from our own domestic resources.
"Wages and salaries "From the budget statement provision has been made for only 20% increase in salaries. All the talk of enhanced salaries effective January 2007 by ministers and NPP propagandists is only nothing but propaganda. Average salary increases in 2007 cannot exceed 20%. The Public Sector wage reforms have taken centre-stage since the budget was read. According to the Finance Minister, the Public Service has 650,000 employees. Out of this number 350,000 are employed by 110 subvented organisations and 300,000 in the civil service. These 650,000 employees' remuneration accounts for 9.5% of GDP. The minister states that the public sector wage bill of 9.5% of GDP is twice as high as what obtains in Nigeria and the Gambia. In spite of this seeming high expenditure on public sector, the government itself and all Ghanaians agree that basically the public service emolument as a per cent of GDP is high because our GDP is low. We need to improve on productivity to improve on our GDP. "The forward to the budget raises the important issue of what is considered problems at the labour front, resulting partly from distorted public sector salary structure which is also poorly administered. To this government intends to use the budget to implement a new comprehensive public sector pay reforms that emphasises equally pay for work of equal worth. This issue is not new. It was the central theme of Ghana's income policy prepared as far back as 1997. This policy is contained in the "final report of Technical Assistance to Facilitate the Development implementation of the medium to long term public sector salaries and wages policy", as at December 1997, price waterhouse. The main report is 210 pages.

Original_Search_Title: 10,000 Driven Out of Homes
Headline: Kenya: 10,000 Driven Out of Homes
Body_Text:
Nairobi — Ten thousand people have been rendered homeless by floods in Budalang'i, Busia District.
Reports indicate that some 5,000 homes were submerged in the area at the weekend and figures could rise as heavy rains continue to pound the area.

Original_Search_Title: Strategy for National Economic Revolution
Headline: Nigeria: Strategy for National Economic Revolution
Body_Text:
Lagos — Apart from the external debt relief from the Paris Club achieved at prohibitive costs, telecommunication explosion which is still exorbitant compared with other sister countries, anti-corruption crusade without positive examples at the leadership level and the railway reconstruction programme for which no time frame has been given, national economic development in the last seven years has not been impressive.
The economic reforms have been controversial, with some of them questionable and others patently counter-productive. Nigerians are still wondering what indeed the reforms have done to improve the quality of their lives.

Original_Search_Title: We'll Support Ibori to Produce Guber Candidate in Delta Before Dec. 9 --Albert Okumagba
Headline: Nigeria: We'll Support Ibori to Produce Guber Candidate in Delta Before Dec. 9 --Albert Okumagba
Body_Text:
INVESTMENT banker, Mr. Albert Okumagba, came into the governorship race in Delta State, few months ago with a bang and since then, political permutations in the state have been altered. Sunday Vanguard spoke with the busy technocrat, last weekend, in Warri and he insisted that his trail-blazing campaign was not a fluke but a merchandise of a long-term plan. All through the over one hour interview, the man looked unruffled and convinced he will emerge as the PDP gubernatorial flag bearer in the December 9 primaries of the party. What gives Okumagba such buoyancy? He spoke on that and other issues. Excerpts:
ALL of sudden, the name, Albert Okumagba, cropped up in the Delta state governorship race, where have you been all this while and where are you coming from?

Original_Search_Title: Over 50 Senators Will Not Retain Their Posts
Headline: Nigeria: Over 50 Senators Won't Return
Body_Text:
Lagos — Over 50 per cent of the present 109 senators in the Upper Chamber of the National Assembly will not retain their posts when the new senate reconvenes in June next year.
The development became clear yesterday when the Peoples Democratic Party (PDP) which produced more than 70 per cent of the incumbent senators held the primary elections to determine its candidates for next year's general elections.

Original_Search_Title: HIV/AIDS Ravages Leave Families in Anguish
Headline: Tanzania: HIV/AIDS Ravages Leave Families in Anguish
Body_Text:
Arusha — World AIDS Day observed on December 1st was an opportunity to remember the more than 25 million people worldwide who have died from AIDS and support the over 38 million people who are currently living with HIV. It was also a time to recommit ourselves to compassionate action and the promise of partnerships that are creating new hope. At family level the devastations are unbearable.
Vumilia (not real names) is an attractive 35- year- old woman. She is a widow and readily admits that her husband passed away due to complications brought about by HIV/AIDS. Vumilia has one daughter, 14 year old Kristy who has just graduated from standard 7 and is awaiting her exam results. Vumilia also lives with her 22-year old step daughter, Laura. In addition, she takes care of two children left behind by her in-laws who passed away due to cases related to HIV/AIDS. They live in the house that was left behind when her husband died in 1998. She has lived there for 16 years.

Original_Search_Title: A Pig for a Name
Headline: Uganda: A Pig for a Name
Body_Text:
Kampala — MONDAY
The Marathon is finally over and though I was not the winner, there were many others behind me. I used my experience from my boxing days to complete the race. The thing is to look around and find motivation for success. I looked around and there were old people, fat people, short people and women! I decided that none of them would win me!

Original_Search_Title: Two More Killed in Floods As State Says It Will Build Roads
Headline: Kenya: Two More Killed in Floods As State Says It Will Build Roads
Body_Text:
Nairobi — Three more people died in floods yesterday as the Government promised to rebuild roads in Northern Kenya in the next 10 days.
Weather experts also predicted that the heavy rains would go on into January.

Original_Search_Title: Aguariavwodo, NDDC boss  reveals- I Wasted Four Years at Home 'Cos My Dad Couldn't Pay My Fees
Headline: Nigeria: Aguariavwodo, NDDC boss  reveals- I Wasted Four Years at Home 'Cos My Dad Couldn't Pay My Fees
Body_Text:
MANAGING Director of the Niger-Delta Development Commission (NDDC), Mr. Emmanuel Aguariavwodo spoke to Saturday Vanguard in Port-Harcourt recently on how he stayed at home for four years after finishing his primary education in 1968 because he had no money to go to secondary school until one day when Olorogun Micheal Ibru heard about his plight and offered him scholarship.
Even after he obtained his first university degree, he still had to sell printing papers in Lagos to survive until he got a job as a journalist. God took control of his life thereafter, as he was compensated with the NDDC job after losing his re-election bid to the House of Representatives. However, he was suspended by President Olusegun Obasanjo some years later but still got his job back. Why was he really suspended and how did he get a clean bill of health? Mr. Aguariavwodo who is in the governorship race in Delta State on the PDP platform answered this and more in this encounter.

Original_Search_Title: Way Out of N-Delta Crises, By 65 Nobel Laureates
Headline: Nigeria: Way Out of N-Delta Crises, By 65 Nobel Laureates
Body_Text:
Lagos — In an apparent internationalisation of the struggles of the people of Niger Delta region, 65 Nobel laureates comprising the Commission of Nobel Laureates on Peace, Equity and Development in the Niger Delta Region of Nigeria have proposed measures to prevent the spiral of deadly violence in the oil-rich yet impoverished Niger Delta region.
The group of laureates has also alereted Nigerians on the dangers of postponing the general elections of 2007.

Original_Search_Title: I Will Take Nigeria to a Place of Glory -- Adamu
Headline: Nigeria: I Will Take Nigeria to a Place of Glory -- Adamu
Body_Text:
Since he assumed as Governor of Nasarawa state, Adamu Abdullahi, a well-heeled lawyer, has consistently carved a niche for himself as planner, a team player and more than anything, manager of resources, conflicts and crisis. Over time, the latter attribute has earned him the appellation of a bridge-builder. He has built bridges reaching to virtually every part of this country. His style of governance in Nasarawa and personal attributes are such that even his foes would not fail to admire. He is so down-to-earth in teachings, pragmatic in thoughts and precisional in focus. This is a man whose maturity helped in transforming Nasarawa to a united cohesive State. His maturity in administration resulted in the construction and completion to time of the state-of-the-art dual highway from Abuja, the Federal Capital to his State, a feat that not many of his colleagues could boast of.
Again, but for his understanding of the interconnection of politics and people, the Master-plan reclaim programme of the Federal Capital Territory, which led to demolition of several houses and consequent displacement of several thousands of people would have become a major social catastrophe. The bridge builder perhaps saw it ahead and channelled part of the urban development programme of his administration towards the boundary between the State and FCT. Today, this part of Nasarawa houses large number of displaced families who are able to continue with their lives by engaging in various economic activities. Governor Abdullahi has stepped out as one of the Presidential aspirants on the platform of the Peoples Democratic Party (PDP). In this interview, he talks about his plans for Nigeria, his vision for the country and what he knows of the PDP and the Presidency.

Original_Search_Title: Woman Candidate Out to Grab Madagascar Presidency
Headline: Madagascar: Woman Candidate Out to Grab Madagascar Presidency
Body_Text:
Antananarivo — With the election of Ms Ellen John-Sirleaf of Liberia as the first woman president in African history, women on the continent are pushing hard in politics. Filled with the concepts of gender equality, African women want to deviate from mere political objects who merely clap and cheer during male-dominated elections.
Madagascar is no exception, where a woman now lines up for the country's hotly contested male-dominated presidential race set for this Sunday.

Original_Search_Title: What We Have Against This Budget - NDC
Headline: Ghana: What We Have Against This Budget - NDC
Body_Text:
The following is the full text of "National Democratic Congress (NDC) Response to the 2007 Budget Statement and Economic Policy of Government".
"Ladies and gentlemen of the Media, the NDC registers its appreciation and gratitude to you for taking time off your busy schedules to honour our invitation to our press conference on the Government's 2007 Budget Statement and Economic Policy presented to Parliament by the Minister of Finance and Economic Planning on 16th November 2006. Given time constraints, our response would highlight a number of significant areas as our contribution to the debate.

Original_Search_Title: Aids Orphans: Making it on Their Own Together - and Thriving
Headline: Africa: Aids Orphans: Making it on Their Own Together - and Thriving
Body_Text:
Nairobi — ­ As the international community readies tobserve the nineteenth World AIDS Day with the theme, “The Promise of Partnerships,” one partnership growing across East Africa is a beacon of hope in the face of the pandemic.
It is a partnership of children orphaned by AIDS and child-headed households ­ who are making it on their own by working together.

Original_Search_Title: Nigeria's GDP Rises By 7.5 Percent
Headline: Nigeria's GDP Rises By 7.5 Percent
Body_Text:
Abuja — Governor of the Central Bank of Nigeria (CBN), Professor Charles Soludo, yesterday disclosed that Nigeria's Gross Domestic Product (GDP), has risen by 7.5 per cent.
Briefing State House Corre-spondents on the outcome of the weekly Federal Executive Council (FEC) meeting, Soludo also disclosed that the nation's inflation rate has fallen by 6.5 per cent.

Original_Search_Title: Nakapiripirit Gets Food Security Scheme
Headline: Uganda: Nakapiripirit Gets Food Security Scheme
Body_Text:
Kampala — NAKAPIRIPIRIT district has launched a food security scheme through which every sub-county will identify a crop to be cultivated as a food reserve.
The project, funded by the National Agricultural Advisory Services (NAADS), will identify crops that performed well in the different sub-counties in the district.

Original_Search_Title: Dr. Kumbuor Batters 2007 Budget - Accuses Gov't On Energy And Wage Issues
Headline: Ghana: Dr. Kumbuor Batters 2007 Budget - Accuses Gov't On Energy And Wage Issues
Body_Text:
THE MAIN opposition party, the National Democratic Congress (NDC) has punched holes in the 2007 budget of the government, presented to Parliament by the Minister of Finance and Economic Planning, Mr. Kwadwo Baah-Wiredu on Thursday November 16.
Delving into all areas of the budget, the NDC accused the government of woefully failing to address two key issues, namely; public sector wage and energy crises, pointing out that, "All that it seeks to do is to postpone them, hoping the good people of Ghana would in no time forget or as usual allow sleeping dogs lie."

Original_Search_Title: Stakeholders' Forum or Good Governance?
Headline: Nigeria: Stakeholders' Forum or Good Governance?
Body_Text:
Yours sincerely was a participant at the last controversial Presidential Stakeholders' Forum on Education at Abuja.
I therefore bear testimony that feverish mass assemblage of people officially talked at (not necessarily talked with!) on policy issues always after discovery of some huge rot under the advertised banner of a stakeholders' forum cannot and should not be substitute for policy formulation and policy implementation by those elected and appointed to govern. Simply put, stakeholders' forum is no substitute for good governance. Stakeholders' forums are assuming notoriety as official platforms for governmental policy grandstanding increasingly aimed at concealing failed governance when the actual expectations of the electorate of those elected and appointed to govern is service delivery, service delivery and service delivery ad infinitum. The concept of stakeholders has been promoted by varying donor agencies and multilateral institutions such as International Monetary Fund (IMF) and the World Bank. This is after much considerable global criticism of the top-down approach of these "development partners" to development issues in developing countries. It was not uncommon for instance for poverty alleviation policies conceived in Washington, D.C. to be rammed down the throats of some poor consumers in Malawi. Stakeholders' forum is a smart but nonetheless top-down device to either legitimise or rationalise policy preferences through pretence at some involvement of the people about their plight. Even at that, it is doubtful if the multilateral institutions which invented this cleaver legitimisation device ever imagined that routine collection and summoning of potpourri of onlookers, mass-dignified as stakeholders would become substitute for genuine governance as it is increasingly the case in Nigeria in recent times. We have had stakeholders' forum on banking sector, forum on aviation sector, forum on census, forum on Niger Delta, forum on textile, forum on cassava, etc, yet the more we look the less we see qualitative value addition for time and resources tied down for hours of alleged stockholding by stakeholders. While on the one hand, stakeholders' forum gives the impression of presidential concern for the sectors under searchlight, your guess is as good as mine as to the efficacy of these unstructured periodic televised sermons on the mountain. For one, these stakeholders' forums often take place after (and not) before abysmal degeneration in the respective sector. The forums are more reactive rather than proactive betraying elementary principle of good governance which is all about envisaging problems and challenges within a broad vision and mission of elected government officials. Judging from the quality of mass media camera coverage of each forum, observers note that the grandstanding has tuned into another public relations exercise under the jack-boot of the President and his Ministers to say we care about the rot even if the problems persist. This observation has been reinforced by the painful fact that every forum is followed by deeper rot in the respective sector instead of advertised promised changes. For instance, the stakeholders' forum on aviation called in the wake of serial air disasters has been followed by greater carnage. Countless gatherings on Niger Delta have been greeted with more deadly raids and hostage takings by the militants. Textile industry and manufacturing sector in general slides from bad to worst under the regime of countless stakeholders' forums. The strikes in Unity schools worsen after a stakeholders' forum and it took good governance via traditional institutions for conflict resolutions to get the striking teachers back to work and not some grandstanding called stakeholders' forum. Of what relevance are mass gatherings that increasingly tie down working executives who should actually be working to solve problems after which the problems get further compounded? Many observers note that the problem with the last census exercise was that governors and other relevant stakeholders who should be working for proper head count at local levels were receiving presidential lectures at "Stakeholders' Forum" in Abuja.

Original_Search_Title: Agip Spends N2.6bn in Agric Intervention Scheme
Headline: Nigeria: Agip Spends N2.6bn in Agric Intervention Scheme
Body_Text:
Port Harcourt — The Nigerian Agip Oil Company Limited and its parent company ENI Group have invested N2.6bn ($20m) in the Agricultural intervention built into its community relations scheme in Nigeria.
The intervention which is executed under the "Green River project," encourages farmers to form cooperative societies in four Niger Delta States of Delta, Imo Rivers and Bayelsa who they train and supply free farming implements.

Original_Search_Title: Cancer Cases On the Rise - GAEC
Headline: Ghana: Cancer Cases On the Rise - GAEC
Body_Text:
Professor Edward H.K. Akaho, Director General of the Ghana Atomic Energy Commission (GAEC) has said within the next 20 years there could be 200 million cancer cases worldwide with 75 per cent coming from developing countries.
He said realising the upsurge of cancer in the country the Commission had requested the International Atomic Energy Agency (IAEA), for assistance to upgrade and expand the Centre for Radiotherapy and Nuclear Medicine at the Korle-bu and the Komfo Anokye Teaching Hospitals and to establish a new one at Tamale to provide care for the patients.

Original_Search_Title: Edo Deputy Speaker Bares It All
Headline: Nigeria: Edo Deputy Speaker Bares It All
Body_Text:
Benin City — Mr. Omo Ezomo is the Deputy Speaker of the Edo state House of Assembly. A very humble and astute politician, his peers defer to him because of his composure and experience, being the only lawmaker in Edo to have made it three times to the House, this time representing Owan West local government council of the state.
Just last Saturday, the National Association of Nigerian Students (NANS) gave him an award as the most performing lawmaker in the state. Saturday Vanguard cornered this politician from the Edo North senatorial district of Edo state for this chat and the soft-spoken legislator revealed how he fell in love with his classmate while he was in secondary school. Somehow, he bowed out of it when he discovered that the girl was only interested in the relationship because of the academic assistance he was rendering to her. He spoke on other issues including why he wants to go the House of Representatives in 2007.

Original_Search_Title: Why LRA Could Not Eat Meat
Headline: Uganda: Why LRA Could Not Eat Meat
Body_Text:
Kampala — THE Lord's Resistance Army, under the leadership of Joseph Kony, blends of Christianity and African traditional beliefs to give its members reason to fight, as seen in Kony's chief priest, Abonga Papa's notes reproduced here.
In the course of the war, Kony would get messages from the spirit known as Lakwena, which he would issue as instructions to his fighters. Abonga Papa, in his capacity as the chief priest, recorded the messages.

Original_Search_Title: Luweero Anglican Bishop Shuffles Clergy
Headline: Uganda: Luweero Anglican Bishop Shuffles Clergy
Body_Text:
Luweero — Anglican Bishop, Rt. Rev. Evans Mukasa Kisekka on Sunday shuffled the diocese leadership.
This was during the annual diocesan council meeting at Luweero Diocesan Guest House.

Original_Search_Title: Economy Dominates Polls
Headline: Madagascar: Economy Dominates Polls
Body_Text:
Improvement of living standards and a smooth transition of political institutions have become the main campaign issues as Madagascar edges closer to D-Day on 3 December.
With 6.3 million Malagasy nationals expected to elect the country's leader for the next five years, political parties have been jostling to position their candidates for president of the Indian Ocean island.

Original_Search_Title: Ajumako Enyan Esiam District Residents Refuse to Pay Property Rate
Headline: Ghana: Ajumako Enyan Esiam District Residents Refuse to Pay Property Rate
Body_Text:
Cape Coast — THE DISTRICT Chief Executive of Ajumako Enyan Esiam District in the Central Region, Mr. Kenneth Obempong, has disclosed that people in his District were refusing to pay property rates, resulting in low revenue mobilization.
He said some residents did not understand the reason why they should pay tax on their personally acquired properties, making it difficult for the Assembly to generate more revenue for development.

Original_Search_Title: Minister Urges Promotion of Nigerian Products
Headline: Nigeria: Minister Urges Promotion of Nigerian Products
Body_Text:
Minister of Commerce, Dr Aliyu Modibbo, has asked Nigerian diplomats to prioritise the marketing and promotion of Nigerian goods, services and resources at their duty posts.
Modibbo was speaking in Berlin, Germany, while presenting promotional materials for his flagship initiative, "Commerce 44", to the staff of the Embassy in Germany at the start of his advocacy visit to that country.

Original_Search_Title: Food Crisis Hits Mpigi
Headline: Uganda: Food Crisis Hits Mpigi
Body_Text:
Mpigi — RESIDENTS of Mpigi district are suffering a serious food crisis. According to the District Production Coordinator, Herman Ssekiwunga the shortage is due to inadequate rains in the previous season and delayed rain for the current season.
"In addition to inadequate rain, vermin and laziness in the community also contributed to the ongoing food shortage," Ssekiwunga told Daily Monitor on Monday.

Original_Search_Title: Sironko Farmers Embrace Exotic Goats for Milk
Headline: Uganda: Sironko Farmers Embrace Exotic Goats for Milk
Body_Text:
Kampala — PEASANT farmers in Sironko and Mbale districts have adapted to keeping British Toggenburg (BT) goats for milk production.
The goats believed to have high nutritious milk are donated by Farm-Africa, a non-government organisation, aimed at reducing poverty amongst farmers in Bugisu region.

Original_Search_Title: Acholi Still Remains Uganda's Food Basket
Headline: Uganda: Acholi Still Remains Uganda's Food Basket
Body_Text:
Twenty years of civil strife in northern Uganda may have destroyed the communities of one of Uganda's most resilient populations, but it certainly never tampered with their spirit and love for hard work.
In situations where you expect thousands of internally displaced people in camps holding out bowls for financial assistance and food portions from sympathisers, the Acholi people either out of desperation or strong will to pick up their pieces and move on, have dived straight into what they do best - farming to sustain themselves.

Original_Search_Title: 2007 - I'll Focus On People -Odili
Headline: Nigeria: 2007 - I'll Focus On People -Odili
Body_Text:
Lagos — RIVERS State Governor and leading presidential aspirant for 2007, Dr. Peter Odili has promised to focus on the people, if elected president.
Dr. Odili stated this a an interactive session with media executives in Lagos yesterday, noting that his government will ensure the resuscitation and change of attitude and respect for the dignity of labour.

Original_Search_Title: IDPs Build Roads But Stay Stuck in Camps
Headline: Uganda: IDPs Build Roads But Stay Stuck in Camps
Body_Text:
Kampala — THE bushy and overgrown roads of Oyam district, once prone to ambushes by the Lord's Resistance Army (LRA) rebels and once a prime source of insecurity, are now one of the glimmers of hope for Oyam residents.
There is an overpowering feeling of fear among displaced communities in northern Uganda who have learnt of the pending truce agreement between the LRA and the Government, but who have not been able to 'return' to their previous lives. The internally displaced persons (IDPs), who have chosen to leave the camps and return to their homes, have found that on top of insecurity, they lack enough food.

Original_Search_Title: Of Oshiomhole, Edo State And 2007
Headline: Nigeria: Of Oshiomhole, Edo State And 2007
Body_Text:
Kaduna — Since its creation over 30 years ago, Edo State had never had it so bad in terms of governance. In the good old days, Edo State always set the pace for others to follow. But in this new dispensation, the seven years of People Democratic Party's administration in the state, led by Governor Lucky Igbenedion is a colossal waste to the state, as billions of monthly allocation accruing to the state have not improved the life of the citizenry.
Past leaders like Professor Ambrose Ali, late Major General George Inih, John Odigie Oyegun and others who have vision worked hard and developed the state. Indeed they developed it to an extent that all the present government needs is to improve on it and bring joy and hope to the people of the state by making education, water and other social amenities accessible and affordable. But today Edo State has been turned into a lame duck that cannot work even at thirties; the vision of our past leaders had been punctured by the PDP-led government to pave way for hunger, unemployment and utter hopeless in the state.

Original_Search_Title: Food Reserve Agency Buys 382,000 Tonnes of Maize
Headline: Zambia: Food Reserve Agency Buys 382,000 Tonnes of Maize
Body_Text:
THE Food Reserve Agency (FRA) has bought 382,000 tonnes of maize as at yesterday, Agriculture and Cooperative Minister Ben Kapita told Parliament.
Mr Kapita said in a ministerial statement that the Government would ensure that all the maize grain from farmers was bought.

Original_Search_Title: Voters' Registration - We Reject This Evil Plot to Rig 2007 Polls
Headline: Nigeria: Voters' Registration - We Reject This Evil Plot to Rig 2007 Polls
Body_Text:
FROM Lagos to Suleja, and on to Abakaliki and other nooks and crannies of Nigeria, there have been widespread complaints and protests by political parties, politicians and other Nigerians concerning the on-going voters registration exercise. The more worrisome has been the steady breakdown of the Direct Data Capture machines imported for the purposes of conducting more credible polls next year.
Today, the parties and politicians point accusing fingers at the Federal Government and the Independent National Electoral Commission (INEC) that the shoddy shows in virtually all the states, are pointers to the fact that the exercise had been originally designed to benefit some people and punish others.

Original_Search_Title: Coping With Life in the City - K'la's Poor
Headline: Uganda: Coping With Life in the City - K'la's Poor
Body_Text:
When I was in secondary school, one of my favourite subjects was geography. There were bits of the subject I disliked, and others that held me spellbound. One topic, rural-urban migration, has stuck in my memory all these years. Here we learnt about how and why people left the countryside and headed for urban settings.
We were told that, lured by 'the lights' they all came in pursuit of a better life, away from the drudgery of their parents' and relatives' peasant existence. The general expectation was that by moving to the city and earning generous salaries and wages, they would ascend the socio-economic ladder and cut their ties to the land.

Original_Search_Title: Udenwa And the Presidential Challenge
Headline: Nigeria: Udenwa And the Presidential Challenge
Body_Text:
Lagos — It has become so apparent that the next president of the Federal Republic of Nigeria after President Olusegun Obasanjo will have a lot of problems to contend with whether he emerges from the South East, South/South or any of the Northern geo-political zones. This is taking into cognizance the emerging controversies that are presently playing itself out in the political firmament of the , nation. The fact that apart from the South West that seems to have accepted that in . the present circumstance they cannot produce' the next president, the remaining five geo-political zones are at each other's throat. And that is an indication that a lot of horse trading would finally come into play on which zone takes a shot at the presidency. The political parties are by their actions not settled on where the next president should emerge from. For now, majority of the political parties have their chairmen coming from the South East but this is not to say the aspirants from the zone nursing presidential dream have gone to the rest.
However, while Nigerians are presently worried about the on-going voters registration exercise of the Independent National Electoral Commission, INEC that appears to be wobbling, Nigerians are also watching the scenario and the hordes of presidential aspirants that have so far thrown their hats into the ring. Most of them possess rare qualities that ought to put them head first among others. This is taking into cognizance their healthy and wealthy backgrounds as well as their standing across the length and breadth of the nation. The fact that they have criss-crossed the nation wooing supporters and putting their score cards on the table are enough reasons to show that the electorate would have a hectic time making a serious choice.

Original_Search_Title: Japanese Govt Donates ICT Gadgets Worth $41,000 to LG
Headline: Nigeria: Japanese Govt Donates ICT Gadgets Worth $41,000 to LG
Body_Text:
Warri — THE Japanese Government, yesterday, at Ogbe-Ijoh, headquarters of Warri South- West Local Government Council of Delta State, donated Information and Communication Technology (ICT) gadgets worth over 41,000 United States Dollars to the council to assist the Women Economic Empowerment Programme of the United Nations Development Programme (UNDP) in the state.
The programme meant to principally support women entrepreneurship and technical skills in selected communities provides relevant and appropriate equipment such as farm implements, cassava and palm oil processing, palm-kernel cracking machines, biogas etc to women groups is being organised in Akwa-Ibom, Delta and Rivers States in Nigeria.

Original_Search_Title: Meat Price Up
Headline: Uganda: Meat Price Up
Body_Text:
Kampala — BEEF, chicken and goat meat prices have gone up due to low supply, a survey has shown.
A kilogramme of beef costs sh3,000 from sh2,700, while goat meat goes for sh4,000 from sh3,500. Chicken costs between sh6,000 and sh15,000, depending on the size.

Original_Search_Title: 'Integrate Concept of Farmer Study Groups'
Headline: Zimbabwe: 'Integrate Concept of Farmer Study Groups'
Body_Text:
Harare — GOVERNMENT has been urged to integrate and institutionalise the concept of farmer study groups and field schools as an alternative agriculture extension method to empower newly-resettled and smallholder farmers.
Apart from encouraging the active involvement of farmers, the methods tap into existing knowledge and the experience in farming among farmers, participants at a recent conference on farmer study group initiatives said.

Original_Search_Title: Surviving With a Fragile Peace
Headline: Uganda: Surviving With a Fragile Peace
Body_Text:
On the rough and bumpy ride from Gulu town to Anaka trading centre about 35kms west of Gulu town, the road is full of signs of life.
Trucks laden with matooke from Paidha in Nebbi district head in the opposite direction to town. Young men energetically peddle bicycles loaded with merchandise, beads of sweat dripping from their faces. People can be seen by the roadside, working in their green fields of groundnuts, beans and cassava. There is even a lone UPDF soldier in his garb walking along the road, not with a gun but a hoe!

Original_Search_Title: Who Succeeds Makarfi in 2007?
Headline: Nigeria: Who Succeeds Makarfi in 2007?
Body_Text:
As 2007 approaches when new governments will emerge at all levels, the people in Kaduna State are already anxious over who would succeed Ahmed Makarfi as governor. The incumbent is believed to have achieved a lot and people are naturally anxious that the size of his shoes might be too large to be easily filled. For many observers, the anxiety is genuine as Makarfi is seen as one of the few outstanding governors.
I must confess for a start that I was among those that felt that the man was too frail to make any impact in that very complicated state. Daily, I kept having the fear that the inherent contradictions in Kaduna would consume the young accountant sooner than later. But more than seven years on, Makarfi has been able to not only keep the lid on this gun-powder of a state, but actually changed the people's lives by executing projects that have uplifted their living standards.

Original_Search_Title: The Stranded Village
Headline: Nigeria: The Stranded Village
Body_Text:
This is a story about a village that got left behind while the rest of the world moved on. In order not to give offence to anyone, alive or dead, I have presented it as a travel tale.
Forty-eight years ago, I became a member of a group in Lagos that was named "The Contemporary Society". It functioned as a sort of standing symposium, drawing its topics, as you may have guessed, from matters related to contemporary affairs.

Original_Search_Title: Military Acts to Help Flood Victims
Headline: Kenya: Military Acts to Help Flood Victims
Body_Text:
Nairobi — It was a sea of hopeful smiles yesterday as military personnel stepped in to help residents of the Coast afflicted by floods.
Sadly, only 3,550 Msambweni residents received relief food supplies from the Kenya Cereals and Produce Board and the Kenya Red Cross Society.

Original_Search_Title: Anglogold Ashanti Donates Oil Palm And Corn Mill
Headline: Ghana: Anglogold Ashanti Donates Oil Palm And Corn Mill
Body_Text:
The Executive Officer of Anglogold Ashanti, Africa Open Pit Mines, Mr. Fritz Neethling, has said that the company will continue to form partnership with its host communities, share their traditions, environment and values, adding that, 'we want the communities to be better off for Anglogold Ashanti having been there'.
He was speaking at the commissioning of a ¢138 million processing plant for oil palm fruit, kennel, cassava and corn to boost its Alternative Livelihood Programmes in the communities within its operational area at Adieyie near Tarkwa last week.

Original_Search_Title: Rural Women Dazzle Audience with Product at Exhibition
Headline: Nigeria: Rural Women Dazzle Audience with Product at Exhibition
Body_Text:
Rural women from Moro local government area of Kwara state yesterday amazed guests with their product at an exhibition organised by the Raw Materials Research and Development Council in Abuja.
The exhibition is meant to showcase finished products from Nigerian raw materials.

Original_Search_Title: Cassava, Sorghum to Drive Agrobusiness
Headline: Kenya: Cassava, Sorghum to Drive Agrobusiness
Body_Text:
Some of East Africa's widely grown crops are among the commodities that will run agro-business in the 21st century.
Stakeholders in the agricultural sector were told on November 1 that sorghum, cassava, soy beans, palm oil and Jathropha curcas, are the five crops that will run agri-business this century.

Original_Search_Title: Ana Marks 25th Anniversary in Grand Style
Headline: Nigeria: Ana Marks 25th Anniversary in Grand Style
Body_Text:
Yenagoa — The celebration of the 25th anniversary of the Association of Nigerian Authors (ANA) at the Gloryland Cultural Centre in Yenagoa the Bayelsa State capital can be described as a reasonable success. Especially looking at the minute human and financial resources available for the Local Organising Committee of the anniversary celebration. However that did not deter the determination with which the committee and other members of ANA Bayelsa, worked to realize their objective. This objective was the successful organization of a befitting anniversary for ANA.
The opening and closing ceremonies were never short of bigwigs in the literary landscape of Nigeria which included the grand old child of the river Nun, Pa Gabriel Okara and Elechi Amadi, our concubine who agreed to share his muse-wife with us.

Original_Search_Title: In Memory of Uganda's Forgotten Heroes
Headline: Uganda: In Memory of Uganda's Forgotten Heroes
Body_Text:
Kampala — Mzee Aurelio Vuje, whose photographic mind earned him the nickname 'camera', is also for the same reason called kamaera, the Madi word for 'it remembers me.' The octogenarian is truly entitled to such names since nothing can be fitting to describe his memory.
For everything Vuje did, there was a substantive reason and when he rummaged through his metallic box that morning, the village knew he was up to something.

Original_Search_Title: Olorogun Oskar Ibru - Wealth is Not Enough
Headline: Nigeria: Olorogun Oskar Ibru - Wealth is Not Enough
Body_Text:
THE best way to make money is through inheritance- I want to leave a good inheritance for Delta." That was Olorogun Oskar Christopher Eyovbierere James Ibru reminiscing on his good fortune. Though it has not always been the silver spoon for him, through dint of hard-work and a great vision, his father Olorogun Michael Ibru built the family name and empire into the colossus it is today. Many know of the family's big businesses, but it would appear that their biggest asset is the land holding. Put simply, if you know the numerous land holdings in Lagos and environs, you probably have not seen anything yet until you get to areas around the ancestral homes of old Mid-West.
On pedigree, there is a lot that can be said about the Ibru family. And that goes back to patriarch of the family, Olorogun Peter Epete Ibru, who taught his children through personal example, the fear of God and the value of perseverance and tenacity of purpose. He was an ordinary dispenser in the colonial times, who through great vision sent his children to some of the best schools of the time. He saw then that knowledge will be power. Another lesson the Ibru clan are well positioned to teach Nigerians is the importance of family unity. Through three generations, the family remains united in ways that are uncommon.

Original_Search_Title: Lagos Promotes Cassava Processing Option
Headline: Nigeria: Lagos Promotes Cassava Processing Option
Body_Text:
Lagos State Commissioner for Agriculture & Cooperatives, Asipa Kaoli Olusanya has stated that the State Government is promoting diversification of processing options of cassava which includes unfermented cassava flour for bread and confectioneries, dry fufu, tapioca and other industrial uses such as starch and dextrose.
He disclosed this at the commissioning of the modern community - based cassava processing centre and distribution of 1000 new improved coconut seedlings at Iragon, Badagry as part of the Agricultural Week celebrations.

Original_Search_Title: Rains Disrupt Food Supplies
Headline: Uganda: Rains Disrupt Food Supplies
Body_Text:
Kampala — FOODSTUFF supplies have been disrupted due to heavy rains that have made roads to rural areas impassable, a weekly survey has revealed.
Traders said commodity prices remained stable but said distribution would be affected if the rains continue.

Original_Search_Title: Coval Cautions Rice Dealers
Headline: Liberia: Coval Cautions Rice Dealers
Body_Text:
The Consumer Volunteers Association of Liberia, (COVAL), has urged rice dealers in the country to remain patriotic by keeping rice sale in accordance with the Commerce Ministry's regulatory price list.
"We've noted that dishonesty to one another was one of the major reasons for our war, and as such, if we wish to end the war finally, Liberians should remain honest in every little thing they do", emphasized COVAL.

Original_Search_Title: EU To Reproduce Agagu's Projects in N/Delta
Headline: Nigeria: EU To Reproduce Agagu's Projects in N/Delta
Body_Text:
Akure — Impressed by the infrastructural and developmental programmes of Governor Olusegun Agagu in Ondo State, the European Union has expressed willingness to replicate the projects and programmes in six states of Niger Delta.
The EU representative, Dr Mariam Abu, who lauded the ingenuity of Agagu in the areas of infrastructures provision, said the next Micro Project Programmes for the nine Niger Delta states (MPP9) would be tailored towards Agagu's initiatives in developing the rural areas.

Original_Search_Title: Okumagba Assures On Even Development
Headline: Nigeria: Okumagba Assures On Even Development
Body_Text:
Warri — PDP governorship aspirant in Delta State, Mr. Albert Okumagba, yesterday, said that no community and tribe, whether in the upland or coastland will be left behind undeveloped if voted into office as governor in 2007.
Mr. Okumagba stated this in Warri, while offering himself to the people for greater service to the state and the country.

Original_Search_Title: Governor Implores Leaders On Hunger
Headline: Rwanda: Governor Implores Leaders On Hunger
Body_Text:
Kigali — The Provincial Governor, Eraste Kabera, has urged local leaders in the province to grow more food crops to fight hunger and poverty. Kabera made the remarks last week during a consultative meeting aimed at laying strategies of implementing and increasing food production especially cassava, to eliminate hunger and fight poverty in the province.
The meeting held at Muhanga district offices was attended by all district mayors, security officials, executive secretaries, provincial agronomists and experts in cassava production.

Original_Search_Title: Vitamin A Fortified Potato to Combat Blindness
Headline: Uganda: Vitamin A Fortified Potato to Combat Blindness
Body_Text:
Kampala — UGANDANS can now combat malnutrition and blindness.
A team of reseachers at Namulonge and Kabanyoro Research Institute have come up with a Vitamin A fortified sweet potato variety. The sweet potato, which is orange, has carotene, the most important source of Vitamin A. Vitamin A is essential for good eyesight and lack of it can cause blindness or, in milder cases, inability to see or drive at night.

Original_Search_Title: Wild Animals Threaten Luweero Farmers
Headline: Uganda: Wild Animals Threaten Luweero Farmers
Body_Text:
Kampala — The National Agriculture Advisory Services (NAADS) programmes which are meant to promote food security in Luweero and Nakaseke districts could be frustrated by wild animals.
"Every other day, warthogs (wild pigs), velvet monkeys and bushbucks plunder farmers' gardens, eating cassava, sweet potatoes, watermelons, cabbages and tomatoes," says John Ngondwe, the vermin control officer. in Luweero and Nakaseke districts

Original_Search_Title: Bunyoro is Still Stuck in the Past
Headline: Uganda: Bunyoro is Still Stuck in the Past
Body_Text:
Kampala — THE Bunyoro region has one of the largest sugar factories in the country at Kinyara. This region has got some of the most fertile soils in Uganda. It grows tobacco on a fairly large scale and has many game reserves and a big game park.
The region has two lakes: Albert and Kyoga. However, the people of Bunyoro remain some of the poorest in Uganda.

Original_Search_Title: Flour Prices Up
Headline: Uganda: Flour Prices Up
Body_Text:
Kampala — Prices of flour have gone up due to the high cost of operating machines by the grain millers, a weekly survey has revealed.
A kilogramme of maize flour was sold at sh900 from sh800, while millet flour went for sh1,500 from sh1,200.

Original_Search_Title: UNIDO Constructs Hydro-Power Project in Enugu
Headline: Nigeria: UNIDO Constructs Hydro-Power Project in Enugu
Body_Text:
Awgu — According to the United Nations statistics, over 2 billion people lack access to modern energy services, whereas the vast majority lives in rural areas of the poorest regions of the world. Among other things, the lack of energy causes social and economic poverty, under-development, unemployment and low living standards. It also contributes largely to under-utilisation of rural human resources, leads to stagnant rural economy and lack of development of value-adding industry.
But according to the United Nations Industrial Development Organisation (UNIDO), which is siting N722 million multi-purpose hydro-power project, located along Enugu-Port-Harcourt express way, "access to energy is a pre-requisite for sustainable development and for fighting poverty. Availability of affordable and sustainable energy is critical to the achievement of the Millennium Development Goals (MDGs)."

Original_Search_Title: Nakaseke Passes By-Law On Food Security
Headline: Uganda: Nakaseke Passes By-Law On Food Security
Body_Text:
